what_it_does: This file is a structured design-language audit/specification of the first fifteen slides (part 1 of 2) of a ConceptV capital-raise investor deck, recording per-slide attention flow, emergent and non-emergent visual patterns, reusable components, and content-slot samples.
observations: Slide titles p-02 through p-15 are treated as one continuous grammatical sentence, each opening with a connective (By/With/Which/But/To/Our/In/That/And).
Findings are explicitly split into 'emergent' higher-order rules (e.g., narrative chain, attention currency, opacity temporality) and non-emergent atoms (geometry, type, padding, color).
A strict shape vocabulary is assigned semantic roles: hexagon = Property Wizard, octagon = Virtual Tours, circle = actor, dashed rectangle = scope boundary, hatch band = thesis conclusion.
Gold is the sole attention signal; near-black is structural; warm gray recedes; rare colors (green-teal, maroon, orange-amber) are reserved for specific semantic meanings.
Reduced opacity (~50%) encodes future or not-yet states, except financial projections where the far-future target year is shown at full saturation.
Two bullet glyphs are distinguished: filled triangle for attributes and arrow for causal/process lists.

Reusable components are catalogued with descriptions and reuse notes, including StatCard, PunchlineCard, PipelineFlowDiagram, SymmetricTwoPanel, NetworkDiagram, ScopeBoundaryBox, QRTile, HatchBandThesisFooter, RoadmapScatterChart, and CompetitiveLandscapeRow.
Content samples include character, word, and line budgets for slots such as page-title, section-head, body-paragraph, stat-number, and button labels.
The deck is described as the 30-page expanded form of a content system that also has 15-page and 1-page compressions.
A V-mark watermark is placed at the bottom-right of every information slide at low opacity.
standouts: The titles-form-one-sentence rule is called the single highest-order design rule in the deck.
Gold is the exclusive attention signal; no other bright accent competes.
Each geometric shape carries a fixed semantic role and is never mixed.
The emergent/non-emergent classification of design findings.
The PunchlineCard pattern that isolates a single hero number as the slide's proof-of-claim.
conventions: Follows a regular schema for findings: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule.
Uses consistent terminology such as 'recipe', 'generator_rule', 'evidence', and 'reuse_note'.
Organizes findings by category: emergent-narrative, emergent-attention, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, zone, chrome-mode, sequence.
Provides content samples with quantitative budgets (chars/words/lines).
Includes Unicode glyphs (▸, →) inside JSON strings to denote bullet types.
Separates cover-mode (full-bleed photo) from information-mode (cream-hatch field) as distinct rendering modes.
concerns: Some color values are approximate ranges (e.g., ~#C8A800) rather than exact tokens.
The 'depth compression' finding is marked as inferred, not directly evidenced in the file.
The hatch-band thesis footer is listed as evidence on p-11 with the note 'implied', which weakens the rule's consistency.
The category 'gradient-shading-opacity' groups two unrelated findings (hatch texture and cream fills).
The file covers only slides 1–15 of a stated 30-page deck, so completeness cannot be verified from this file alone.
Long prose strings may complicate automated querying and extraction.
notes: A dense, rule-encoded design autopsy of a pitch deck that treats color, shape, opacity, and bullets as a strict semantic grammar.
questions: Are the color values exact extracted values or visual estimates?
Was the continuous-sentence title chain an intentional authorial device in the original deck, or an interpretive imposition?
What schema or version governs this ledger entry format?
How does the second part of the deck continue or complete the narrative and visual system?
Are the components intended to be implemented as code components, or are they purely design documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an extracted design review/specification of the deck, which aligns with its path under review/ingest/_extracted.
apparent_themes: Semantic visual grammar — form encodes meaning.
Attention economics — gold as a scarce, routed signal.
Compression and expansion of content depth.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to reverse-engineer the investor deck into an explicit, generative design system whose rules are strong enough to reproduce or extend the deck consistently.
novelty: The explicit treatment of slide titles as a single grammatical sentence as the deck's primary rhetorical structure, and the rigorous mapping of shapes and colors to fixed semantic roles, are unusual for a design audit.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, rule-based decomposition of a pitch deck's visual language, including emergent narrative, attention, and semantic patterns, reusable component definitions, and content-slot budgets that a fused system could use to generate or audit similar decks.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a structured design-language audit of the first fifteen slides of a ConceptV capital-raise investor deck, documenting slide-level attention flow, emergent visual-semantic rules, reusable components, and content-slot samples.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to extract and codify the deck's design grammar so it can be reproduced, extended, or compared across 30-page, 15-page, and 1-page compression levels.
